_docs.purpose: Single source of truth for the CLI-visible knob surface: every config key, dotted config path, CLI flag, embedder (napi/engine request) field, and external-tool flag that a zzop message is allowed to name. Consumed by lib/mapper.js (KNOWN_KEYS, runtime drift warnings) AND by crates/engine/tests/rule_contracts/'s reference-validation meta-test (CHECK A / CHECK B), so both sides of the contract read the exact same data — a config key or flag can never drift between what the CLI accepts and what the engine's own rule/warning messages are allowed to recommend.
_docs.configKeys: Moved verbatim from lib/mapper.js's former KNOWN_KEYS constant — the fixed-shape config object's known keys, by scope. See mapper.js's own comment for the 'warn on drift, never reject' contract this backs. Path resolution bases: `trees[].root` is passed through by the JS mapper as a literal string for the native engine to resolve against its own process cwd, while the Rust-hosted mapper (crates/config) resolves it to absolute against a caller-supplied `base_dir` — a documented CLI/Rust deviation, see crates/config/src/mapper/paths.rs's doc comment; `overlays` entries (both top-level and `trees[].overlays`) are instead resolved by both dialects against the OWNING TREE's own root, never `base_dir`. For the zzop-mcp server specifically (verified against packages/mcp/src/tools.rs + crates/summary/src/paths.rs + crates/config/src/lib.rs): `base_dir` is NOT a fixed value — it is either the tool call's own (already-absolutized) tree root (`analyze_repo`, and `cross_repo`'s config-free `paths` mode), or the directory containing the (already-absolutized) `configPath` (`cross_repo`'s config-first mode). The absolutizing step itself is the actionable part: a RELATIVE `path`/`paths`/`configPath` tool argument is resolved by the MCP HOST PROCESS against ITS OWN current working directory (`std::path::absolute`, i.e. wherever the client happened to launch/configure the `zzop-mcp mcp` process) — a directory the calling MCP client cannot see or rely on. Consequently: always pass ABSOLUTE paths for the `path`/`paths`/`configPath` tool arguments in MCP usage, and prefer an absolute `trees[].root` when authoring a `zzop.config.jsonc` consumed via `configPath`, so no part of the join depends on the server process's unseen cwd. `packs.extraDirs` is the config-file dialect for adding a DSL rule-pack directory — do not write `packsDir` in a `zzop.config.jsonc` (that's the embedder/napi request field a host application sets directly; see `embedderFieldShapes.packsDir` for its own back-reference).
_docs.rulesValues: A `rules.<ruleId>` entry (see mapper.js's rules-processing loop, ~line 394, and crates/config/src/mapper/options.rs's mirror for the Rust-hosted front end — both dialects accept EXACTLY the same two shapes and reject everything else identically) is either of exactly two shapes: (1) a severity/disable STRING token, or (2) an OBJECT `{ severity, exclude }` (the `ruleObject` shape above) whose `severity` key (optional) takes the same string token and whose `exclude` key (optional) is an array of path substrings/globs. The string form maps through `SEVERITY_ALIASES`: `critical`/`error`/`err`/`high` -> critical, `warning`/`warn`/`medium` -> warning, `info`/`information`/`note`/`low` -> info, and `off`/`none`/`disable`/`disabled` disables the rule entirely — `"off"` (or any of its aliases) is how a rule is disabled via config, as opposed to the embedder dialect's `disabled_rules`/`disabledRules` list. ANY other value shape — a bare boolean (`false` included — there is no boolean dialect; writing `"ruleId": false` to disable a rule is invalid, use `"off"`), a number, an array, `null`, or an object without `severity`/`exclude` — is rejected with a load-time `ConfigError` naming the rule id (`rules.<ruleId> must be a severity string (e.g. "warn"/"off") or an object ({ "severity": ..., "exclude": [...] }).`), never silently ignored and never treated as disabling the rule.
_docs.configPaths: Every valid DOTTED spelling of a nested config key (derived from configKeys), in the form an author would actually write it in a message: `<parent>.<child>` for a fixed-shape nested object (e.g. `git.since`), `<parent>[].<child>` for a per-entry field of an array-of-objects (e.g. `trees[].root`). Deliberately excludes `rules.<id>.severity`/`rules.<id>.exclude`: `<id>` is an open-keyed rule id, not enumerable here (a message naming a specific rule id under `rules` is checked as a JSON-snippet shape instead, see the rule_contracts meta-test's CHECK B doc).
_docs.cliFlags: The real flags packages/cli/bin/zzop.js's parseArgs() recognizes, enumerated by reading that file (not assumed) — both the long and short spelling where both exist. `--force` is init-only, `--mode`/`--kind` are init-adapter-only, `--json`/`--config` are shared by run and endpoint, every other flag is run-only (see parseArgs' scoped-flag cross-check). LANE: every entry here describes the `@zzop/cli` npm package's own command (`zzop` — `zzop.js`'s parseArgs()), never the Node-free `zzop-mcp` binary. `zzop-mcp` has no flags at all — it dispatches on SUBCOMMANDS (`analyze`, `cross`, `endpoint`, `contract`, `version`, `mcp`, `help` — see packages/mcp/src/main.rs's USAGE constant), and a `zzop-mcp`-binary-only user (no Node/npm involved) reading this list would otherwise believe these `zzop` flags apply to their binary; they do not.
_docs.embedderFields: napi/engine request-surface field names (crates/facade/src/lib.rs's AnalyzeRequest / packages/native/index.js) that legitimately appear in a zzop message as 'the embedder dialect' — i.e. the field name a HOST APPLICATION embedding @zzop/native directly (not through the zzop CLI/config file) would set. Every native rule's disable-hint already says this explicitly: 'Disable via config `rules: {...}` (embedders: `disabled_rules`)'. Kept to exactly the fields a message in the scanned dirs actually references today (see the rule_contracts meta-test's CHECK B doc comment for the verification) — extend only when a new message legitimately needs to name another embedder field, not preemptively.
_docs.embedderFieldShapes: One-line JSON shape per embedderFields entry, so an embedder field named in a zzop message is discoverable from this file alone (a consumer holding only the binary + this resource would otherwise see a bare name with no shape). Documentation only — no code path consumes this section (lib/mapper.js reads configKeys; the rule_contracts meta-test reads the flat lists); keep its key set mirroring embedderFields exactly.
_docs.externalToolFlags: Flags of OTHER tools (never zzop's own) that a zzop message legitimately mentions when explaining an OUTSIDE cause for a symptom. Today that's exactly git's own `--depth` (a shallow clone, e.g. `git clone --depth=1`) and `--unshallow` (`git fetch --unshallow`, the fix) — both from crates/metrics/src/diagnostics.rs's thin-git-history warning. Kept minimal: enumerated by scanning the real tree, not pre-populated with every flag a tool happens to have.
_docs.allowlistedTokens: Literal tokens that are not config paths/keys at all but legitimately sit inside CHECK B's +/-120-char 'near the word config' window anyway — a filename convention (`zzop.config.jsonc`, the CLI's own config file, referenced as plain prose text today but allowlisted preemptively so a future backtick-quoted mention doesn't spuriously fail) or an unrelated identifier/header name that happens to share a sentence with a config-disable hint. Each entry's source is documented in the rule_contracts meta-test's CHECK B doc comment, not just here. `ir.io` — packages/mcp/src/tools/definitions.rs's analyze_repo/cross_repo descriptions name it as the raw-io OUTPUT field their own shaped MCP reply omits (the same co-location pattern as `crossLayer.unresolvedConsumes`: an output field, not an input config knob, mentioned near a config-honoring clause in the same sentence).
_docs.mcpToolTokens: The zzop-mcp host's tool-surface vocabulary: every tools/call ARGUMENT name (packages/mcp/src/tools/definitions.rs inputSchema properties, enumerated by reading that file) plus the reply fields its self-description text backticks (`config`, `configWarnings`, `ruleOverridesApplied`, `bucketKeySites`, `ir`). A DIFFERENT dialect from config-file keys — vouched as its own section (not allowlist noise) since packages/mcp/src joined the reference-validation scan set (2026-07-17), so a tool description naming a nonexistent argument fails CHECK B the same way a bad config key does. `ir` was added when analyze_repo/cross_repo's descriptions started naming the raw engine field their shaped MCP reply deliberately omits (see this file's `allowlistedTokens` doc for the paired `ir.io` entry — the dotted form is checked as an allowlisted token, not through this list, since CHECK B's dotted-token branch does not consult mcpToolTokens). `packsLoaded` was added when `validate_rule_pack`'s description started pointing authors at it (2026-07-17): the tool validates one pack's shape in isolation, so a pack `id` collision with a bundled/other pack — which replaces the loser WHOLE — is invisible to it and only surfaces at load time via the engine's `packsLoaded` report (plus its shadow warning), which the description now names by backtick. `architecture` and `gitWindow` were added (2026-07-17, git-signal disclosure-asymmetry fix batch) when analyze_repo's description started naming its own new reply field (`architecture`: a compact, capped `{pain, topRecommendation, criticalTop}` summary of the facade's `health`/`recommendations`/`critical` output, present only when git signals ran) and the forwarded engine echo field `gitWindow` ({recentDays, since}).
_docs.treeFields: Per-field type/requiredness for `configKeys.tree` (one entry of a `trees[]` array), read from crates/config/src/mapper.rs + crates/config/src/mapper/validation.rs (the Rust-hosted front end's own fail-fast gates — the JS mapper's `trees[]` handling, packages/cli/lib/mapper.js ~line 600, is the same shape): `root` — string, REQUIRED, non-empty; a path resolved against `base_dir` (see the path-resolution _docs paragraph below). `sourceId` — string, OPTIONAL; when omitted, defaults to the tree's OWN raw `root` string exactly as written (e.g. a tree with `root: "./api"` and no `sourceId` gets `sourceId: "./api"` — NOT the resolved absolute path and NOT just the root's basename). That is the CONFIG-file dialect default (this `trees[]`/`roots` mapping); a config-less/bare-path analyze request (no config file, no `trees[]` in play at all — e.g. an embedder calling `@zzop/native`'s `analyze()`/`analyzeTrees()` directly with a bare `root`) instead defaults an empty/missing `sourceId` to the analyzed root DIRECTORY's own basename, applied at the facade chokepoint every host funnels through (`apply_source_id_default`, `crates/facade/src/analyze.rs`, D14 2026-07-17) — lossy `to_str` fallback: the given root string verbatim if the basename can't be decoded (e.g. a bare drive root). `overlays` — string[], OPTIONAL; each entry is a path to a Mode B adapter envelope JSON file, resolved against THIS TREE'S OWN root (never `base_dir`, never the config file's directory — see `configKeys`'s own overlay-resolution note); an unreadable or non-JSON overlay is skipped with a warning, never a hard error. `mountedAt` — string, OPTIONAL; a whole-tree gateway/ingress prefix shorthand, validated to start with `/`, contain no `://` scheme, no `{}` placeholder, and no whitespace (a violation is a load-time `ConfigError`, not a warning). `mounts` — array of `{ dir, at }` objects, OPTIONAL; `dir` must be tree-relative (must NOT start with `/`) and use forward slashes only (no backslash); `at` follows the same validation as `mountedAt`. `hosts` — string[], OPTIONAL; each entry a BARE host (no `://` scheme, no `/` path separator, no whitespace) — a full URL is rejected. Separately, `trees` itself (the array's own top-level key, sibling to `roots`) accepts the literal string `"auto"` instead of an array: this expands to one tree per workspace package (`sourceId` = each package's name), detected from `pnpm-workspace.yaml` or `package.json`'s `workspaces` field (crates/config/src/workspaces.rs / packages/cli/lib/workspaces.js) — a monorepo shortcut that turns the cross-layer join on with no hand-authored `trees[]` array. `roots` vs `trees: "auto"`: `trees: "auto"` always scans the CONFIG FILE'S OWN directory for workspace members; `roots` never steers auto mode (it has zero effect once `trees: "auto"` is in play). A config carrying BOTH is a conflict, not a silent merge — auto wins, `roots` is dropped, and the config gets one `configWarnings` entry naming that `roots` was ignored (`crates/config/src/workspaces.rs`'s `expand_auto_trees`).

embedderFieldShapes.adapterOverlays: NormalizedEnvelope[] — mode-B io overlays (partial envelopes, typically io + fragment channels for a few files) merged on top of native analysis for the tree; an invalid overlay is skipped with a warning
embedderFieldShapes.disabled_rules: string[] of rule ids — the snake_case spelling native-rule disable-hints use for the same list as disabledRules
embedderFieldShapes.disabledRules: string[] of rule ids — those rules never run/report
embedderFieldShapes.packsDir: string | string[] — DSL rule-pack directory path(s), loaded in order (a later same-id pack replaces the earlier one whole); explicit null opts out of every DSL pack, bundled included. Config-file dialect: `packs.extraDirs` — `packsDir` is the embedder/napi request field, not a valid zzop.config.jsonc key.
embedderFieldShapes.suppressions: { rule, path?, glob? }[] — drops findings for one rule (path = substring match, glob = full-path anchored; glob wins when both are set)
